prerequisites.action: Clear errors in both ST2.0 and the app
Confirm controller and all device firmware is up to date
Ensure nothing is currently running on the controller before starting
Advise customer not to start anything on the controller while investigating

errors.meaning: Communication with the IGBus device timed out.

steps.action: Power cycle the controller long enough to clear cache
steps.detail: Overnight recommended with batteries removed from controller

steps.action: Verify heads are connected and properly assigned
Reseat head connections if needed
If the error persists, replace under warranty if applicable or have the customer purchase a replacement

errors.meaning: The electrical part of communication with the IGBus device was detected to be corrupted on the controller side.

steps.action: Power cycle the controller long enough to clear cache
steps.detail: Overnight recommended with batteries removed from controller

steps.action: Inspect cable — may be a bad cable

errors.meaning: The logical part of communication with the IGBus device was detected to be corrupted on the controller side (ACK error).

steps.action: Power cycle the controller long enough to clear cache
steps.detail: Overnight recommended with batteries removed from controller

steps.action: Inspect cable — may be a modified cable at controller or bad cable

errors.meaning: Not enough current was detected on the line.

steps.action: Check diagnostic results and verify current on line
steps.detail: Expect approximately 30 per head

steps.action: Verify controller terminal connections for that line are secure
Systematically isolate the problem device on the line if issue persists
If the issue persists and further testing is needed, we can send out an extra cable for troubleshooting purposes
If the error persists, replace under warranty if applicable or have the customer purchase a replacement

notes.text: Open errors typically mean nothing is detected on the line — check cabling for disconnects or cut wires

errors.meaning: The line was detected by the hardware to be shorted (extremely high current).

steps.action: Check ST2.0 logs for any prior errors before this issue
steps.detail: Look for Water, Valve, or Rotor errors — these are likely the root cause

steps.action: Have customer check for modified cables at the controller
Verify current on line via diagnostic results
steps.detail: Expect approximately 30 per head

steps.action: Systematically isolate the problem device on the line if issue persists
If the error persists, replace under warranty if applicable or have the customer purchase a replacement

notes.text: A prior Water, Valve, or Rotor error in the logs is likely the root cause of this error

errors.meaning: Too much current was detected on the line.

steps.action: Check ST2.0 logs for any prior errors before this issue
steps.detail: Look for Water, Valve, or Rotor errors — these are likely the root cause

steps.action: Have customer check for modified cables at the controller
Verify current on line via diagnostic results
steps.detail: Expect approximately 30 per head

steps.action: Systematically isolate the problem device on the line if issue persists
If the error persists, replace under warranty if applicable or have the customer purchase a replacement

notes.text: A prior Water, Valve, or Rotor error in the logs is likely the root cause of this error

errors.meaning: Failed to measure the current on the line.

steps.action: Have customer check for modified cables at the controller
Verify current on line via diagnostic results
steps.detail: Expect approximately 30 per head

steps.action: Systematically isolate the problem device on the line if issue persists

errors.meaning: The controller was not able to talk to the driver chips used for IGBus communication.

steps.action: Escalate to engineering

errors.meaning: A timeout occurred when waiting for a motor movement to complete. This is usually aliased to another error.

steps.action: Listen for audible clicking or humming — if present, the head has failed and should be replaced
Power cycle the controller long enough to clear cache
steps.detail: Overnight recommended with batteries removed from controller

steps.action: Follow leads if other errors are discovered during investigation

errors.meaning: The sprinkler claims to have a rotor error (probably real).

steps.action: Listen for audible clicking or humming — if present, the head has failed and should be replaced
Power cycle the controller long enough to clear cache
steps.detail: Overnight recommended with batteries removed from controller

steps.action: Test different mapping points and have customer confirm nozzle turns to verify rotor functionality
If issue only occurs in quick-run or schedules, delete and set up the head again
If restoring mapping causes the issue to return, manually re-map the head again
If the error persists, replace under warranty if applicable or have the customer purchase a replacement

notes.text: For S3.13: Manually try to turn the sprinkler while it's performing the homing routine on boot-up. If the sprinkler is NOT making the telltale click that comes with a broken gearbox, it could be failing to home due to a buildup of debris in or on the rotator cap.

errors.meaning: The sprinkler claims to have a valve error (probably real).

steps.action: Listen for audible clicking or humming — if present, the head has failed and should be replaced
If valve is stuck open and water will not stop, replace the head immediately and have customer turn off water until replaced
Power cycle the controller long enough to clear cache
steps.detail: Overnight recommended with batteries removed from controller

steps.action: Test valve functionality with mapped points
If issue only occurs in quick-run or schedules, delete and set up the head again
If restoring mapping causes the issue to return, manually re-map the head again

errors.meaning: The position reported by the sprinkler did not match expected after valve home operation. Indicates a dropped or early terminated movement. Currently known to appear randomly.

steps.action: Power cycle the controller long enough to clear cache
steps.detail: Overnight recommended with batteries removed from controller

errors.meaning: The current for the valve motor was never detected to fall. Electrical error usually.

steps.action: In the shape editor, move between 2 different points and have someone on-site confirm the head turned
If still reporting the same error when trying to quick-run, replace the head
If the error persists, replace under warranty if applicable or have the customer purchase a replacement

errors.meaning: The position reported by the sprinkler did not match expected after valve movement operation. Indicates a dropped or early terminated movement. Currently known to appear randomly.

steps.action: In the shape editor, move between 2 different points and have someone on-site confirm the head turned
If still reporting the same error when trying to quick-run, replace the head

errors.meaning: Failed to home initially but was eventually able to get the valve closed.

steps.action: In the shape editor, move between 2 different points and have someone on-site confirm the head turned
If still reporting the same error when trying to quick-run, replace the head

errors.meaning: The position reported by the sprinkler did not match expected after rotor home operation. Indicates a dropped or early terminated movement. Currently known to appear randomly.

steps.action: Power cycle the controller long enough to clear cache
steps.detail: Overnight recommended with batteries removed from controller

notes.text: For S3.13: Manually try to turn the sprinkler while it's performing the homing routine on boot-up. If the sprinkler is NOT making the telltale click that comes with a broken gearbox, it could be failing to home due to a buildup of debris in or on the rotator cap.

errors.meaning: The current for the rotor motor was never detected to fall. Electrical error usually.

steps.action: Complete normal troubleshooting steps, then discharge the controller overnight

errors.meaning: The position reported by the sprinkler did not match expected after rotor movement operation. Indicates a dropped or early terminated movement. Currently known to appear randomly.

steps.action: Try adding drop-down points
Escalate to engineering if the issue persists

errors.meaning: The controller failed to scan the head.

steps.action: Look through the logs — a previous electrical or water error on that head is likely the cause. If found, the head needs to be replaced
Power cycle the controller long enough to clear cache
steps.detail: Remove batteries for Controller 2 as well. Overnight recommended

steps.action: If no previous errors are found, back up the system and delete the head in ST2.0
Perform diagnostics: Scan & Update — if successful, something was corrupt and the head will need to be recalibrated and remapped
If unsuccessful, escalate to engineering for confirmation — replacement of the head is likely required
steps.detail: Escalate to engineering

steps.action: If the error persists, replace under warranty if applicable or have the customer purchase a replacement

errors.meaning: The controller failed to update the head.

steps.action: Look through the logs — a previous electrical or water error on that head is likely the cause. If found, the head needs to be replaced
Power cycle the controller long enough to clear cache
steps.detail: Remove batteries for Controller 2 as well. Overnight recommended

steps.action: If no previous errors are found, back up the system and delete the head in ST2.0
Perform diagnostics: Scan & Update — if successful, something was corrupt and the head will need to be recalibrated and remapped
If unsuccessful, escalate to engineering for confirmation — replacement of the head is likely required
steps.detail: Escalate to engineering

steps.action: If the error persists, replace under warranty if applicable or have the customer purchase a replacement

errors.meaning: The controller attempted to use an IGBus device which was not enabled.

steps.action: Escalate to engineering

errors.meaning: The pressure from the head was invalid or corrupted.

steps.action: Power cycle the controller long enough to clear cache
steps.detail: Overnight recommended with batteries removed from controller

steps.action: Run calibration and make sure expected throw matches forward/backward and that RCO is standard
If throw still accurately tracks mapping, the error can be ignored
If persistent, disable pressure sensor reporting in ST2.0
If calibration fails, disable pressure sensor reporting in ST2.0 and copy calibration from the closest head of the same model

errors.meaning: Tried to use the pressure sensor but none was detected. Often a false positive.

steps.action: Power cycle the controller long enough to clear cache
steps.detail: Overnight recommended with batteries removed from controller

steps.action: Run calibration and make sure expected throw matches forward/backward and that RCO is standard
If throw still accurately tracks mapping, the error can be ignored
If persistent, disable pressure sensor reporting in ST2.0
If calibration fails, disable pressure sensor reporting in ST2.0 and copy calibration from the closest head of the same model

notes.text: Sprinkler versions older than 2.5 (1.52) do not have pressure sensors.

errors.meaning: The controller attempted to use the sprinkler probe results but they were invalid. Should never happen.

steps.action: Escalate to engineering

errors.meaning: Tried to operate the valve but it was not indexed. Should never happen.

steps.action: Escalate to engineering

errors.meaning: The device had a fatal error on the sprinkler side. Currently only a water error.

steps.action: Replace the head
If the error persists, replace under warranty if applicable or have the customer purchase a replacement

errors.meaning: The assigned device and actual device types did not match. Requires manual intervention to fix.

steps.action: Remove devices showing the error and re-add them, with customer help identifying which devices they are
Restore mapping and calibration from backup
steps.detail: Backup is automatically created when deleting devices

steps.action: If backup is corrupt and error returns after re-adding, manually calibrate and re-map from scratch

notes.text: Deleting and re-adding devices one at a time generally removes the need for customer assistance in identifying them

errors.meaning: Controller 2 is not connecting to the network.

steps.action: Has the home WiFi network changed?
steps.yes_note: If yes, skip to the network reconnection steps
steps.no_note: If no, continue with all steps in order

steps.action: Make sure the black antenna on top of the controller box is present and securely fastened
Soft reset the controller by holding the green button for 1 second — it will attempt to automatically reconnect to the home network
Clear cache: have customer disconnect transformer, remove both batteries on the bottom left corner of the controller board, and let it sit overnight
Reset controller: hold the red button, then hold the green button for 1 second and release it, tap the blue button, wait for the cycling lights to finish, then release red
steps.detail: See reference video: Reset_C2.MOV

steps.action: Delete the network information saved in ST2.0: Controls > System > "Wipe Network Cache"
Broadcast Bluetooth on the controller: hold the red button for 6 seconds
Have the customer disconnect from the controller in the IrriGreen App: More (bottom right) > "Disconnect from controller" (bottom)
Have the customer tap "Add Controller" and either scan the QR code or manually enter the controller number and password from the sticker on the controller box
Follow the prompts — when the app says it's offline and asks to continue with Bluetooth, tap yes and connect to the network using the password on the controller box sticker
Let the controller sync — when prompted, connect to the desired home network, ensuring it is a 2.4 GHz band
When online, manually update the controller and attached devices through ST2.0 if needed
If the error persists, replace under warranty if applicable or have the customer purchase a replacement

notes.text: Reference video: Reset_C2.MOV — demonstrates the button sequence for a full controller reset

branching_flow.description: After steps 1–3, attempt network connection (steps 5–9), then branch on success.

checkpoint.question: Were you able to connect to the IrriGreen network (broadcast as "IrriGreen-[Controller#]")?

no.label: No — could not connect
no.action: Factory reset the controller: first create a backup in ST2.0 via Controls > Backups > Create Backup. Then perform the full reset (step 4): hold the red button, hold the green button for 1 second and release it, tap the blue button, wait for the cycling lights to finish, then release red.

then_checkpoint.question: Were you able to connect to the IrriGreen network (broadcast as "IrriGreen-[Controller#]")?

errors.meaning: Controller 3 is not connecting to the network.

steps.action: Has the home WiFi network changed?
steps.yes_note: If yes, skip to the network reconnection steps
steps.no_note: If no, continue with all steps in order

steps.action: Delete the network information saved in ST2.0: Controls > System > "Wipe Network Cache"
Have the customer disconnect from the controller in the IrriGreen App: More (bottom right) > "Disconnect from controller" (bottom)
Press and hold the middle button (WiFi symbol above it) for ~5 seconds — ensure the digital display reads "Bluetooth On"; repeat if it says "Bluetooth Off"
Tap the leftmost button (hamburger menu above it), scroll with the dial until the display reads "IG Network", press the dial to confirm, then press again when display reads "Confirm"
In their IrriGreen app, have the customer tap "Add Controller" and either scan the QR code or manually enter the controller number and password from the sticker on the controller box
Follow the prompts — when the app says it's offline and asks to continue with Bluetooth, tap yes and connect to the network using the password on the controller box sticker
Let the controller sync — when prompted, connect to the desired home network, ensuring it is a 2.4 or 5 GHz band
When online, manually update the controller and attached devices through ST2.0 if needed
If the error persists, replace under warranty if applicable or have the customer purchase a replacement

branching_flow.description: After completing steps 1–6, a confirmation branch determines next steps.

checkpoint.question: Were you able to connect to the IrriGreen network (broadcast as "IrriGreen-[Controller#]")?

no.label: No — could not connect
no.action: Soft reset the controller: use a flathead screwdriver to remove the topmost black plate in the IrriGreen controller box, then press the small circular reset button for about 1 second

then_checkpoint.question: Were you able to connect to the IrriGreen network (broadcast as "IrriGreen-[Controller#]")?

no.label: No — still could not connect
no.action: Factory reset the controller: first create a backup in ST2.0 via Controls > Backups > Create Backup. Then have the customer push in and hold the dial, push in and hold the WiFi button, then push in the reset button for 1 second — all buttons can then be released. Wait for the controller to come back online.

then_checkpoint.question: Were you able to connect to the IrriGreen network (broadcast as "IrriGreen-[Controller#]")?

errors.meaning: Head is throwing water shorter than mapped, resulting in under-watered areas.

steps.action: Ensure the starting point is the longest throw of the run and that it does not immediately drop to a closer point
Flush the nozzle
steps.detail: Reference: https://support.irrigreen.com/en/sprinkler2/flushing

steps.action: Flush the riser: while no water is coming out of the head, remove the nozzle and filter inside, then let water bubble out of the head for ~30 seconds by starting shape editor or performing a Quick Run
Re-calibrate the head — review the successful calibration and ensure the curve matches; adjust the curve if needed and input new calibration values
If throw is still short, disable AutoWater and redo calibration

errors.meaning: Head is throwing water farther than mapped, resulting in over-spray.

steps.action: Ensure the starting point is the longest throw of the run and that it does not immediately drop to a closer point
Flush the nozzle
steps.detail: Reference: https://support.irrigreen.com/en/sprinkler2/flushing

steps.action: Flush the riser: while no water is coming out of the head, remove the nozzle and filter inside, then let water bubble out of the head for ~30 seconds by starting shape editor or performing a Quick Run
Re-calibrate the head — review the successful calibration and ensure the curve matches; adjust the curve if needed and input new calibration values
If throw is still long, disable AutoWater and redo calibration

errors.meaning: Throw jumps too far or too short, resulting in tracking that resembles a circular saw blade — highly abnormal pattern.

steps.action: Ensure the starting point is the longest throw of the run and that it does not immediately drop to a closer point
Add a dropdown point roughly opposite the starting point, with a popup point as close to the dropdown point as possible
steps.detail: This gives the valve a chance to re-home and water a chance to repressurize

steps.action: Flush the nozzle
steps.detail: Reference: https://support.irrigreen.com/en/sprinkler2/flushing

steps.action: Flush the riser: while no water is coming out of the head, remove the nozzle and filter inside, then let water bubble out of the head for ~30 seconds by starting shape editor or performing a Quick Run
If AutoWater is enabled, disable it
Re-calibrate the head — review the successful calibration and ensure the curve matches; adjust the curve if needed and input new calibration values
If this head has a history of pressure sensor errors and is generation 2.5 (1.52) or newer, enable the "Disable Pressure Sensor" mitigation
steps.detail: With pressure sensor disabled, automatic calibration is no longer available. Copy calibration from a nearby head of the same model, or request factory default calibration values from engineering if no other head is available to copy from

errors.meaning: Each revolution, the throw gets consistently shorter or longer, resulting in tracking that resembles a spiral.

steps.action: Ensure the starting point is the longest throw of the run and that it does not immediately drop to a closer point
Add a dropdown point roughly opposite the starting point, with a popup point as close to the dropdown point as possible
steps.detail: This gives the valve a chance to re-home and water a chance to repressurize

steps.action: Flush the nozzle
steps.detail: Reference: https://support.irrigreen.com/en/sprinkler2/flushing

steps.action: Flush the riser: while no water is coming out of the head, remove the nozzle and filter inside, then let water bubble out of the head for ~30 seconds by starting shape editor or performing a Quick Run
If AutoWater is enabled, disable it
Re-calibrate the head — review the successful calibration and ensure the curve matches; adjust the curve if needed and input new calibration values
If this head has a history of pressure sensor errors and is generation 2.5 (1.52) or newer, enable the "Disable Pressure Sensor" mitigation
steps.detail: With pressure sensor disabled, automatic calibration is no longer available. Copy calibration from a nearby head of the same model, or request factory default calibration values from engineering if no other head is available to copy from

errors.meaning: A sprinkler head or smart valve is not being found during Test & Detect.

steps.action: Run diagnostics in ST2.0 (Test & Update)
If possible, check the current on a head that was discovered — expect ~30 per head and smart valve. If current is too low for the number of connected devices, there is a connection issue
steps.detail: Walk the customer or contractor through reseating connections and letting them fully dry if moisture is found. Advise engineering a solution to have the connection hang from the top or side of the valve box so it won't sit in pooled water

steps.action: If current is close to expected and the customer is having trouble adding the device, remote into the app and enable the correct number of valves in More > System Setup — then try a Quick Run on the new smart valve to confirm it works
If a sprinkler head is still not discoverable, have the customer move it to a location where another head is successfully installed, or run a spare sprinkler cable above ground for testing purposes

errors.meaning: The job was manually aborted.

notes.text: This code indicates the job was manually cancelled by someone using the IrriGreen app — not a fault or hardware issue. No troubleshooting required unless cancellation was unintentional.

errors.meaning: Customer is experiencing unexpected Autowater behavior — schedules being skipped, too many or too few revolutions, or unexpected watering patterns.

steps.action: Ask if any third-party rain or soil sensors are connected, and double-check in the logs
steps.detail: Third-party rain or soil sensors can overrule the data gathered from Weather Underground, which may explain unexpected Autowater behavior

steps.action: Explain how Autowater works
steps.detail: Autowater pulls weather data from Weather Underground using the address on file for the controller and compares it to the calculated water 'debt' (based on grass type, soil type, and direct sun amount listed for each zone). If the water debt is less than the rainfall reported since the last schedule, OR if sufficient rain is expected within the next 24 hours, the schedule will be skipped and the customer will be notified if notifications are enabled. If the debt is greater than reported rainfall, the schedule will run additional revolutions per head until the debt is balanced. It may be worth asking if the customer would like help enabling notifications.

steps.action: Verify the correct address is listed for the controller in ST2.0: Controls > System > under Salesforce
If the address is incorrect or missing, update it in Salesforce
steps.detail: Click 'Open in Salesforce' at the bottom of the Salesforce bubble in ST2.0. Under DeployedatLocationLead, remove the current field and update it with the customer after confirming their address on file is correct. Save changes in Salesforce and refresh ST2.0 to confirm the correct address appears.

steps.action: Ensure correct grass type, soil type, and sun exposure are selected for each zone
If the customer is still unhappy with Autowater performance, explain that Weather Underground data may not be accurate for their region
steps.detail: Options to suggest: (1) Invest in a rain sensor for more accurate local precipitation data — Rain Bird is a good option to recommend. (2) Disable Autowater and manually set the number of revolutions per zone for more direct control — if they go this route, make sure they understand they'll want to turn off schedules manually when it has rained or is expected to rain before the next schedule.

errors.meaning: Customer is not receiving Autowater skip alerts, error notifications, or other app notifications.

steps.action: Make sure the IrriGreen app is up to date
steps.detail: Android: check the Play Store. Apple: check the App Store.

steps.action: Make sure the app is signed into an IrriGreen account
Ensure notifications are enabled for the IrriGreen app in the device's system settings
steps.detail: iOS: Settings > Apps > IrriGreen > Notifications. Android: Settings > Apps > IrriGreen > Notifications.

errors.meaning: Step-by-step guide for replacing the riser, spring, filter, and nozzle on a Sprinkler 3 using the service kit.

steps.action: Remove the nozzle by turning the green cap counter-clockwise while holding the riser firmly with the other hand — once unthreaded it should pull out with little to no resistance
Remove the mesh filter from the base of the riser
Remove the spring from the base of the riser
steps.detail: Bend the bottom of a yard flag to create a small hooked tool. Pull the riser up until it rests around the base of the head, then slide the hooked tool down into the riser toward the arm the spring is connected to at the bottom. Use the hook to push the spring hook off the arm, then spin the tool until the spring hook falls out of the riser.

steps.action: Repeat the process to remove the bottom hook of the spring from the arm inside the base of the head
Connect the new spring to the base of the head
steps.detail: The new spring may have one hook slightly larger than the other — that larger side should face down, connecting to the base of the head.

steps.action: Slide in the new riser — it will only slide in when the grooves are lined up, so rotate the riser if it does not slide in
Connect the new spring to the arm at the bottom of the new riser using the hooked tool
Connect the new cap to the head and screw clockwise until firmly secure
Place the new mesh filter into the riser, making sure it sits flush against the bottom of the riser, right on top of the arm holding the spring

Connect the new nozzle — it will only slide in when the ports line up with their cutout in the riser, so rotate if needed. Once seated, hold the riser firm with one hand and turn the cap clockwise until tightened with the other.
Redo calibration for this head

errors.meaning: Installation schematic for the IrriGreen smart valve.
Installation schematic for the IrriGreen Sprinkler 3.
